pattern: When only the distinct-letter count must drop, greedily sacrifice the rarest letters first because eliminating a letter costs exactly its frequency.
hints: Deleting some letters until at most k kinds remain means choosing which letters to wipe out entirely; partial deletion of a letter never helps.
Removing a letter costs all of its occurrences, so to lose the fewest characters you remove the letters with the smallest frequencies.
Count each letter's frequency, and if there are more than k distinct letters, sort frequencies ascending and sum the smallest (distinct - k) of them.

edge_inputs.description: k larger than distinct count needs nothing
uneven frequencies, keep the most common

stress.description: random short strings over the full alphabet with varied k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(16, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randrange(1, 17)
    return [s, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minDeletion(self, s, k):
        from itertools import combinations
        from collections import Counter
        c = Counter(s)
        letters = list(c.keys())
        d = len(letters)
        if d <= k:
            return 0
        best = None
        # choose which letters to keep (k of them), delete the rest
        for keep in combinations(letters, k):
            ks = set(keep)
            removed = sum(c[ch] for ch in letters if ch not in ks)
            if best is None or removed < best:
                best = removed
        return best
